comments.author: nicogef
comments.text: feat: export gate definitions from skill frontmatter ## Context The DAS `gate` command (nicogef/documentation-as-code#225) currently hardcodes step-to-phase mapping and check definitions in `core/src/commands/gate.ts`. Superpowers skills already define gate criteria per workflow step (e.g., brainstorming requires SRs before planning, writing-plans requires HLRs/DRs before implementation). ## Proposal Move gate policy definitions from DAS into superpowers skills: 1. Each skill declares its exit criteria in a structured format (e.g., a `gates:` block in skill frontmatter) 2. DAS reads gate defin

comments.author: ganningBest
comments.text: Superpowers is hard to use for prompt-engineering-heavy work when TDD validates shape instead of content quality - [x] I searched existing issues and this has not been proposed before ## What problem does this solve? Superpowers works well for deterministic code work: Java/Python codebases, scripts, CLI behavior, and other engineering tasks where the expected behavior can be tested through executable code. But for prompt-engineering-heavy work, I am running into a serious test-boundary problem. In these workflows, the deliverable is often structured generated content, not executable behavior d

comments.author: pixeru
comments.text:
Support Hermes Agent as a Superpowers harness

I’d like to add another user case for Hermes Agent support.

My use case is using Hermes Agent as my main coding harness, especially for larger game development / Unreal Engine workflows where I want the agent to follow a stronger engineering methodology instead of improvising each session. Superpowers already gives that structure in other harnesses, but Hermes users currently have to either manually port skills, copy/paste instructions into SOUL.md, or delega

comments.author: jorgen-k
comments.text: subagent-driven-development: no inline-vs-dispatch threshold — small, tightly-coupled changes pay fan-out overhead for isolation they don't need - [x] I searched existing issues and this has not been proposed before *Closest prior art, checked open and closed: the cost-tiering stack (#1744 / #1717 / #1716) fixes which **model** each SDD role runs on, and #1747 adds the **reasoning-effort** dimension of the same problem. #1835 is about dispatch **mechanism** (inline Agent vs Workflow fan-out). All three optimize how cheaply a dispatched subagent runs. This proposal is a different, prior axis: *

comments.author: gpitrella
comments.text: [RFC] Persistent memory across Superpowers agents - [x] I searched existing issues and this has not been proposed before ## What problem does this solve? When switching between agents or sessions, context doesn't travel with you. Project decisions, preferences, and important details need to be re-explained every time — across sessions and across tools (Claude, Cursor, Codex, Gemini). ## Proposed solution An optional MCP-based integration that makes context portable across all supported agents. Completely opt-in, zero impact on existing workflows. I've been experimenting with this approach and

comments.text: document-skills plugin loads all skills from repo instead of only declared ones in marketplace.json ## Description When installing the `document-skills` plugin via `/plugin install document-skills@anthropic-agent-skills`, **all 17 skills** from the repository are loaded, instead of only the **4 skills** declared in `marketplace.json`. ## Expected Behavior According to `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json`, the `document-skills` plugin declares only 4 skills: ```json { "name": "document-skills", "skills": [ "./skills/xlsx", "./skills/docx", "./skills/pptx", "./skills/pdf" ] } ``` Only these 4 skill

comments.author: ThepExcel
comments.text: Plugin Marketplace installs extra skills when plugin source is repo root (./) ## 🐞 Issue: Plugin Marketplace installs extra skills when `source` is repo root (`./`) ### Summary When installing a skills bundle via Plugin Marketplace (e.g. `plugin marketplace add anthropics/skills`), the installer installs **all skills found under the plugin `source` directory**, not only the skills explicitly listed in the `"skills"` array. This results in many unintended skills being installed. --- ### Expected behavior Given the following configuration: "skills": [ "./skills/xlsx", "./skills/docx", "./skills/

comments.author: harishwtf
comments.text: Add saas-motion-ideation skill: pre-production & creative ideation for SaaS marketing motion design ## Proposal A skill for **pre-production and creative ideation** of SaaS marketing motion design pieces — the thinking that happens *before* any animation or video production begins. This fills a gap in the current skills library. Existing skills cover frontend design, web artifacts, and document creation, but nothing addresses the **creative strategy and storyboarding** phase of marketing content. ## What it does Guides Claude through the full pre-production process for SaaS feature launch vide

comments.author: just2majic
comments.text: Windows compatibility: skill-creator scripts fail (subprocess PATHEXT, cp1252 encoding, select on pipes) ## Summary Running `python -m scripts.run_loop` (or `run_eval` directly) on native Windows Python 3.14 hits three compatibility issues, all rooted in Unix-first assumptions in `scripts/run_eval.py` and adjacent files. The third is the blocker — without it, the optimizer cannot evaluate any query on Windows. ## Environment - OS: Windows 11 Pro (10.0.26200) - Python: 3.14.3 (native Windows, not WSL) - Claude CLI: installed via npm at `~/AppData/Roaming/npm/claude.cmd` - skill-creator: latest

comments.author: tazmad
comments.text: skill-creator description-optimisation loop: recall=0% on every iteration, including literal slash-command queries ## Summary When running `scripts/run_loop.py` (or `scripts/improve_description.py`) against a skill that already has a defined slash-command name, every iteration scores **precision=100%, recall=0%, accuracy=50%** — including queries that are the literal slash-command invocation of that skill. The score is identical across iterations regardless of how much the candidate description changes, so the loop produces no usable signal for description tuning. ## Environment - skill-creato

comments.author: alvingarcia
comments.text: skill-creator: trigger eval writes {skill}-skill-{hash} command files into the live project's .claude/commands/, polluting the skill registry of concurrent sessions ## Summary `skill-creator`'s trigger evaluation (`skills/skill-creator/scripts/run_eval.py`) tests description variants by writing synthetic command files named `{skill_name}-skill-{8hex}.md` directly into the **user's live project** `.claude/commands/` directory. With the default `--num-workers 10`, up to ~10 of these files exist simultaneously during the eval window. Every concurrent Claude Code session rooted at that project see

comments.author: nhosler
comments.text: Windows: eval/optimize harness broken — cp1252 read_text() crash + nested claude -p WinError 10038 silently zeroes all results ## Environment - OS: Windows 11 - Python 3.14.4 - `skill-creator` from `anthropics/skills` (example-skills plugin), run via `python -m scripts.run_loop` Two Windows bugs make the eval / description-optimization harness unusable; the second is the dangerous one because it **fails silently and reports plausible-looking but meaningless metrics**. ## Bug 1 — `parse_skill_md` crashes on any non-cp1252 SKILL.md (blocking) `scripts/utils.py:9` reads SKILL.md with no explicit

comments.author: zaesche
comments.text: skill-creator: silent benchmark failures (layout mismatch, inverted delta), broken trigger evals on Windows, and skill shadowing in trigger evals Six reproducible issues in `skills/skill-creator`, found while auditing and testing the skill end-to-end. Roughly in order of impact: ## 1. SKILL.md directory layout is silently incompatible with aggregate_benchmark.py SKILL.md instructs subagents to save to `<workspace>/iteration-<N>/eval-<ID>/<config>/outputs/` and to put `grading.json` "in each run directory" — a flat layout with no `run-N` level. `aggregate_benchmark.py` however skips every confi

comments.author: griffithsbs
comments.text: skill-creator: eval-viewer escapeHtml is not attribute-safe and is applied inconsistently (display-path XSS) ### Summary The skill-creator eval-viewer (`skills/skill-creator/eval-viewer/viewer.html`) assembles HTML by string concatenation and assigns it with `innerHTML` in `renderGrades` and `renderBenchmark`, relying on a helper `escapeHtml()` at each interpolation point. Two gaps let attacker-influenced data reach the DOM as markup: 1. **`escapeHtml()` is not attribute-safe.** It is implemented with the `textContent` → `innerHTML` round-trip, which escapes `<`, `>`, and `&` but **not** `"` o

comments.author: WGlynn
comments.text: Are external skill contributions welcome? Proposing critical-qa (self-adversarial review before "done") The skills here (skill-creator, mcp-builder, webapp-testing) read as general tools rather than one-off demos, so I wanted to ask directly: do you accept skill contributions from outside Anthropic, and if so is a PR with a SKILL.md folder following ./template the right path, or do you prefer an issue first? The skill I would contribute is critical-qa. Before declaring a code change complete, it runs a short self-adversarial pass: it asks hostile-reviewer questions across several fixed categor

comments.author: ejprice
comments.text: mcp-builder: evaluation.py scores 0/N against any real MCP server (TextContent not JSON serializable, swallowed into fabricated tool errors) ## Summary `skills/mcp-builder/scripts/evaluation.py` (the Phase-4 evaluation harness) silently fabricates a tool-execution error for **every tool call against every real MCP server**, so the driven agent sees nothing but errors and the evaluation scores 0/N. The failure is invisible unless you dig into what the model was actually shown. ## Root cause `connections.py:70` returns `result.content` from `call_tool()` — which for a real MCP server is `list[mc

comments.author: agentupskill
comments.text: Feature request: add coordinator.domains: frontmatter to claude-api SKILL.md (for spec-coordinator tag-routing) ## Context A separate skills framework — [Agent Upskill's spec-coordinator](https://github.com/agentupskill/skills-core/tree/main/spec-coordinator) — uses an SME registry to route cross-domain spec requests to relevant skills. The registry has two routing layers: 1. **Tag-match (deterministic)** — picks skills whose `coordinator.domains: [...]` frontmatter overlaps query tokens (confidence 1.0). 2. **Sonnet classifier (fallback)** — scores skills against the query (threshold 0.6 for

comments.author: optimization2026
comments.text: # Add optional `skill-index.json` for large skill catalogs and plugin packs # Add optional `skill-index.json` for large skill catalogs and plugin packs ## Summary Codex skills work well when a user has a small number of focused skills. But as users install more skills and plugins, a new problem appears: ```text routing deduplication overlap detection skill ownership multi-skill pack navigation context budget control ``` A large catalog should not require the agent to load many full `SKILL.md` files just to decide which one is relevant. This issue proposes an optional compact `skill-index.json`

comments.author: jonmartinAU
comments.text: Adapt Playwright skill for Claude Code Convert the Playwright CLI skill from Codex ecosystem references to Claude Code equivalents. Replace CODEX_HOME with CLAUDE_HOME, update paths from ~/.codex to ~/.claude, rename agents/openai.yaml to agents/claude.yaml, and install to ~/.claude/skills/playwright/.

comments.author: Andrej1707
comments.text: Skill Orchestrator: meta-skill for deduplicating and routing large Codex skill catalogs Hi OpenAI / Codex team, I built an instruction-only Codex skill called **Skill Orchestrator** that acts as a lightweight routing layer for large skill catalogs. The problem I ran into: When a user installs many Codex skills and plugins, several skills can overlap semantically. Examples: app polish vs frontend design, webapp testing vs browser/playwright skills, game playtest vs playtest-debug-loop, broad GitHub routing vs CI-specific skills. This can cause unnecessary context usage, duplicate skill loading,

But as users install more skills and plugins, a new problem appears: ```text routing deduplication overlap detection skill ownership multi-skill pack navigation context budget control ``` A large catalog should not require the agent to load many full `SKILL.md` files just to decide which one is relevant. comments.author: seulch
comments.text: ForgeCat profiles for OpenAI skills Hi OpenAI skills team, I’m working on ForgeCat, a tool for making agent profiles such as skills, agents, rules, commands, hooks, and MCP configs easier to install across different agent tools.
